recapPrompt.prompt: In a min-heap, what kind of value sits on top?

choices.label: The smallest value
choices.feedback: Correct. A min-heap keeps the smallest item on top.

choices.label: The largest value
choices.feedback: Not this one. That is the max-heap rule.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words when you would choose a min-heap instead of a max-heap.
reflectionPrompt.placeholder: I would choose a min-heap when...

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the heap clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main heap clue for each kind of goal.
hints: Min-heap means the smallest item is easiest to reach.
Max-heap means the largest item is easiest to reach.
Top-k often keeps the weakest current survivor easy to remove.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main heap clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck what min-heap, max-heap, and top-k heap choices each mean.

blanks.label: Main clue for many top-k heap problems
expectedValues: keep weakest of best easy to remove
